words.def: the act or process of finishing something; the state of being finished and complete
words.example: the completion of the new hospital building

words.def: made of many different things or parts that are connected; difficult to understand
words.example: a complex problem/issue/process/system

words.def: made of many different things or parts that are connected; difficult to understand
words.example: a complicated issue/process/system

words.def: one of several parts of which something is made
words.example: the different organizations involved in the design of the various components

words.def: to write music
words.example: Mozart composed his last opera shortly before he died.

words.def: a person who writes music, especially classical music
words.example: Verdi was a prolific composer of operas.

words.def: a thing consisting of two or more separate things combined together
words.example: compounds derived from rainforest plants

words.def: including all, or almost all, the items, details, facts, information, etc., that may be involved
words.example: a comprehensive list of addresses

words.def: to have somebody/something as parts or members
words.example: comprise something, The collection comprises 327 paintings.

words.def: that must be done because of a law or a rule
words.example: English is a compulsory subject at this level.

words.def: the ability to direct all your effort and attention on one thing, without thinking of other things
words.example: This book requires a great deal of concentration.

words.def: an idea or a principle that is connected with something abstract
words.example: concept of something, the concept of social class

words.def: a feeling of worry, especially one that is shared by many people
words.example: concern about something/somebody, Villagers expressed concern about the level of traffic.

words.def: worried and feeling concern about something/somebody
words.example: Concerned citizens can have a huge impact.

words.def: made of concrete
words.example: a concrete floor

words.def: a person’s behaviour in a particular place or in a particular situation
words.example: The sport has a strict code of conduct.

words.def: a large official meeting, usually lasting for a few days, at which people with the same work or interests come together to discuss their views
words.example: The hotel is used for exhibitions, conferences and social events.

words.def: to admit, especially formally or to the police, that you have done something wrong or illegal
words.example: After hours of questioning, the suspect confessed.

words.def: the feeling that you can trust, believe in and be sure about the abilities or good qualities of somebody/something
words.example: The president's actions hardly inspire confidence.

words.def: a situation in which people, groups or countries disagree strongly or are involved in a serious argument
words.example: The violence was the result of political conflicts.

words.def: difficult to understand; not clear
words.example: The instructions on the box are very confusing.

words.def: a state of not being certain about what is happening, what you should do, what something means, etc.
words.example: The announcement caused a lot of confusion.

words.def: aware of something; noticing something
words.example: conscious of something, She's very conscious of the problems involved.

words.def: as a result; therefore
words.example: This poses a threat to agriculture and the food chain, and consequently to human health.

words.def: the protection of the natural environment
words.example: to be interested in wildlife conservation

words.def: opposed to great or sudden social change; showing that you prefer traditional styles and values
words.example: the conservative views of his parents

words.def: great in amount, size, importance, etc.
words.example: The project wasted a considerable amount of time and money.

words.def: much; a lot
words.example: The need for sleep varies considerably from person to person.

words.def: the act of thinking carefully about something
words.example: An employer is legally bound to give due consideration to the request.

words.def: always behaving in the same way, or having the same opinions, standards, etc.
words.example: a consistent approach to the problem

words.def: always the same
words.example: Her work has been of a consistently high standard.

words.def: a secret plan by a group of people to do something harmful or illegal
words.example: conspiracy (to do something), a conspiracy to overthrow the government

words.def: happening all the time or repeatedly
words.example: There were constant interruptions.

words.def: all the time; repeatedly
words.example: Fashion is constantly changing.

words.def: to build or make something such as a road, building or machine
words.example: construct something, The building was constructed in 1993.

words.def: the process or method of building or making something, especially roads, buildings, bridges, etc.
words.example: Construction began this year and will take approximately 18 months.

words.def: to go to somebody for information or advice
words.example: consult somebody, If the pain continues, consult your doctor.

words.def: a person who knows a lot about a particular subject and is employed to give advice about it to other people
words.example: a technology/design consultant

words.def: the act of using energy, food or materials; the amount used
words.example: the production of fuel for domestic consumption (= to be used in the country where it is produced)

words.def: belonging to the same time
words.example: We have no contemporary account of the battle (= written near the time that it happened).

words.def: a competition in which people try to win something
words.example: to hold a singing/talent contest

words.def: an official written agreement
words.example: All employees have a written contract of employment.

words.def: to give something, especially money or goods, to help achieve or provide something
words.example: Do you wish to contribute?

words.def: a gift or payment that is made to a person or an organization in order to help pay for something
words.example: All contributions will be gratefully received.

words.def: causing a lot of angry public discussion and disagreement
words.example: a highly controversial topic

words.def: public discussion and argument about something that many people strongly disagree about, think is bad, or are shocked by
words.example: to arouse/cause controversy

words.def: the quality of being useful, easy or suitable for somebody
words.example: We have provided seats for the convenience of our customers.

words.def: the way in which something is done that most people in a society expect and consider to be polite or the right way to do it
words.example: the rigid social conventions of Victorian Britain

words.def: tending to follow what is done or considered acceptable by society in general; normal and ordinary, and perhaps not very interesting
words.example: conventional behaviour/morality

words.def: to change or make something change from one form, purpose, system, etc. to another
words.example: convert something, They took just nine months to convert the building.

words.def: to make ideas, feelings, etc. known to somebody
words.example: convey something, Colours like red convey a sense of energy and strength.

words.def: completely sure about something
words.example: Sam nodded but he didn't look convinced.

words.def: that makes somebody believe that something is true
words.example: a convincing argument/explanation/case

words.def: to deal successfully with something difficult
words.example: I got to the stage where I wasn't coping any more.

words.def: most important; main or essential
words.example: core subjects (= subjects that all the students have to study) such as English and mathematics

words.def: connected with a large business company
words.example: corporate finance/profits/tax

words.def: a large business company
words.example: multinational corporations

words.def: a long narrow passage in a building, with doors that open into rooms on either side
words.example: His room is along the corridor.

words.def: the clothes worn by people from a particular place or during a particular historical period
words.example: an exhibition of Victorian costumes

words.def: a small house, especially in the country
words.example: a thatched cottage

words.def: a group of people who are elected to govern an area such as a city or county
words.example: a town council

words.def: one of a number of areas that some countries are divided into, each with its own local government
words.example: rural counties west of the Mississippi

words.def: the ability to do something dangerous, or to face pain or opposition, without showing fear
words.example: He showed great courage and determination.

words.def: the reporting of news and sport in the media
words.example: media/newspaper/press coverage

words.def: a line on the surface of something where it has broken but not split into separate parts
words.example: This cup has a crack in it.

words.def: an accident in which a vehicle hits something, for example another vehicle, usually causing damage and often injuring or killing the passengers
words.example: a car/plane crash

words.def: the act or process of making something that is new, or of causing something to exist that did not exist before
words.example: Job creation needs to be the top priority.

words.def: the use of skill and imagination to produce something new or to produce art
words.example: Creativity and originality are more important than technical skill.

words.def: a living thing, real or imaginary, that can move around, such as an animal
words.example: respect for all living creatures

words.def: an arrangement that you make, with a shop for example, to pay later for something you buy
words.example: to get credit

words.def: all the people working on a ship, plane, etc.
words.example: crew members

words.def: connected with or involving crime
words.example: criminal offences/activities
